varieties.name: Buchanan
varieties.origin: Developed by University of Sydney from CIMMYT (Mexico) germplasm, released 2017
varieties.traits: APH (Australian Prime Hard), spring wheat; Day-length insensitive, short season (90-110 days), disease resistant to spot blotch, stripe rust, stem rust, leaf rust, and yellow spot

varieties.name: Suncentral (SUN972V)
varieties.origin: Australian Grain Technologies (AGT), tested as SUN972V
varieties.traits: APH quality, hybrid breeding; Quicker maturing than parent Suntop, mid-season variety
varieties.yield_notes: Significantly higher yields than other APH varieties in same planting window

varieties.name: Sunmaster (SUN972P)
varieties.origin: Australian Grain Technologies, tested as SUN972P
varieties.traits: APH quality, mid-season maturity; Wide adaptation, similar to Suntop characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: Out-yields parent Suntop by 6-9% across NSW and Queensland

varieties.name: Sunchaser (SUN843E)
varieties.origin: Australian Grain Technologies, tested as SUN843E
varieties.traits: APH quality, mid-season; Wide adaptation, lower screening risk than Suntop

varieties.name: Sunprime
varieties.origin: Australian Grain Technologies
varieties.traits: APH quality wheat; Heat tolerant variety, good disease resistance

varieties.name: Coolah (V07176-69)
varieties.origin: Australian Grain Technologies, tested as V07176-69
varieties.traits: APH quality in northern NSW/Queensland; Higher disease resistance than predecessors

varieties.name: Cutlass
varieties.origin: Australian breeding program
varieties.traits: AH (Australian Hard) quality; Excellent heat tolerance characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: High yield potential maintained under heat stress

varieties.name: AGT Condo
varieties.origin: Australian Grain Technologies
varieties.traits: Premium milling wheat; Outstanding heat tolerance, good disease resistance package
varieties.yield_notes: High yield maintained under heat stress

varieties.name: LRPB Flanker
varieties.origin: LongReach Plant Breeders
varieties.traits: AH quality wheat; Good heat tolerance characteristics

varieties.name: Mace
varieties.origin: Australian Grain Technologies
varieties.traits: AH/APH quality depending on zone; Wide adaptation, heat tolerant

varieties.name: Scepter (RAC2182)
varieties.origin: Australian Grain Technologies, derived from Mace
varieties.traits: APH/AH quality depending on region; Similar adaptation to Mace, improved stripe rust resistance, YLS and CCN resistance

varieties.name: Leverage
varieties.origin: Australian Grain Technologies, 2023 release
varieties.traits: APH quality, mid-slow maturity; EGA Gregory genetics through parent Coolah, MRMS stripe rust and yellow leaf spot resistance
varieties.yield_notes: Exceptional high yield - beating competitors by more than 10% in early planting
varieties.availability: AGT Affiliates, Queensland seed retailers (2024-25 classification)

varieties.name: Baxter
varieties.origin: Established Queensland variety
varieties.traits: APH quality; Similar maturity to Sunvale, moderate disease resistance

varieties.name: Kennedy
varieties.origin: Queensland breeding program
varieties.traits: APH variety; Quick maturing, good stripe rust resistance

varieties.name: Khapli (Emmer Wheat / Triticum dicoccum) 🏆
varieties.origin: Indus Valley Civilization (10,000+ years), traditional cultivation in Maharashtra-Karnataka border, known as 'Sadak' regionally
varieties.traits: Emmer wheat (tetraploid - A & B genomes, lacks D genome); Long basmati-like grains, hulled wheat, red-brown color, drought and saline tolerant, pest resistant, low gluten (5-6%), low glycemic index (GI 55)
varieties.yield_notes: Lower than modern wheat but resilient in marginal soils
varieties.availability: Organic stores in India, Maharashtra/Karnataka farmers, online specialty retailers (Organic Atman, Daana, Vintage Farmers)

varieties.name: Bansi (Durum Wheat / Triticum durum) 🏆
varieties.origin: Traditional Indian durum variety, documented by Howard & Khan (1910), indigenous to northern India
varieties.traits: Durum/macaroni wheat (tetraploid), heirloom variety; Hardy, pest resistant, drought tolerant, free-threshing, climate resilient
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yields, grown using traditional methods
varieties.availability: Northern India farmers (Punjab, Haryana), traditional seed networks, Daana organic suppliers, specialty heirloom seed sources

varieties.name: Kathia (Durum Wheat) 🏆
varieties.origin: Traditional variety from Haryana and Punjab, documented in Howard catalog (1910)
varieties.traits: Durum wheat (tetraploid), indigenous heirloom; Pest resistant, disease resistant, grown using traditional farming methods, sustainable choice

varieties.name: Lokwan (Bread Wheat) 🏆
varieties.origin: Traditional variety cultivated across India, heritage grain
varieties.traits: Bread wheat (Triticum aestivum), open-pollinated heirloom; Good quality grain, adaptable, higher gluten than emmer/durum heirlooms (8-10%)
varieties.availability: Widely grown across India, local grocery stores, heritage grain suppliers

varieties.name: Sona Moti (Golden Pearl) 🏆
varieties.origin: Traditional variety from Madhya Pradesh and Rajasthan, heritage grain
varieties.traits: Bread wheat, heirloom variety; Golden grain appearance, traditional farming methods, chemical-free cultivation

varieties.name: Paigambari (Indian Dwarf Wheat) 🏆
varieties.origin: Developed by Indus farmers ~4500 years ago using 's' gene from chromosome 3D, pre-Green Revolution dwarf. Also known as Sugar-Free Wheat, Gilgit Wheat, Multani, Gundu Godi
varieties.traits: Hexaploid bread wheat (pre-modern dwarf variety), ancient dwarf; Round kernels (vs long oval modern wheat), reduced protein vs modern varieties but lower gluten, significantly higher folic acid (267% more minerals, 40% more protein/fat than modern wheat)
varieties.yield_notes: Lower yields than modern hybrids, nearly extinct

varieties.name: Chandausi (Semi-hard Bread Wheat) 🏆
varieties.origin: Western Uttar Pradesh, named after town of Chandausi. Documented by Howard & Khan (1910), Triticum aestivum subspecies
varieties.traits: Bread wheat (hexaploid), heirloom variety; Semi-hard texture, traditional cultivation, adapted to Indo-Gangetic plains
varieties.yield_notes: Traditional yields from pre-Green Revolution era

varieties.name: Daudkhani (Triticum aestivum-hordeiforme) 🏆
varieties.origin: Western Madhya Pradesh, documented by Howard & Khan, indigenous subspecies. Also known as Bhal wheat in Gujarat's Bhal region
varieties.traits: Bread wheat subspecies (hexaploid), heirloom; Slightly higher protein, more gluten and beta-carotene than common varieties, traditional cultivation
varieties.yield_notes: Traditional yields, lower than modern hybrids

varieties.name: HD 2967
varieties.origin: ICAR-Indian Institute of Wheat and Barley Research (IIWBR), developed post-Green Revolution
varieties.traits: Bread wheat, semi-dwarf hybrid; High-yielding, disease resistant, widely adapted, shorter stature

varieties.name: HD 3226 (Pusa Yashasvi)
varieties.origin: Indian Agricultural Research Institute (IARI), modern variety
varieties.traits: High-yielding bread wheat; Resistant to yellow rust and brown rust, excellent disease package, Indo-Gangetic Plains adaptation
varieties.availability: IARI Delhi, northern India seed networks

varieties.name: HD 3086 (Pusa Gautami)
varieties.origin: IARI New Delhi
varieties.traits: Semi-dwarf bread wheat; Good resistance to leaf rust and stripe rust, adapted to irrigated regions, good lodging resistance
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding in northern irrigated areas

varieties.name: PBW 343
varieties.origin: Punjab Agricultural University
varieties.traits: Semi-dwarf bread wheat, high-yielding hybrid; High adaptability, consistent yields, excellent rust resistance, timely sown conditions

varieties.name: PBW 550
varieties.origin: Punjab Agricultural University
varieties.traits: High-yielding bread wheat; Good disease resistance, widely adapted in Punjab-Haryana belt

varieties.name: Lok 1
varieties.origin: ICAR breeding program
varieties.traits: Drought-tolerant bread wheat; Drought tolerant, suitable for arid and semi-arid regions, good stability in low-rainfall areas

varieties.name: DBW 303 (Karan Vandana)
varieties.origin: ICAR-Directorate of Wheat Research
varieties.traits: Drought-tolerant bread wheat; Drought tolerant, requires less water, resistant to major fungal diseases, early maturing
varieties.yield_notes: Leading variety in eastern wheat belt

varieties.name: Sharbati (MP 1203)
varieties.origin: Madhya Pradesh, premium variety. Durum type wheat (Triticum durum)
varieties.traits: Premium durum wheat; Long slender grains with golden hue, soft texture, sweet taste
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yields but high market value
varieties.availability: Madhya Pradesh markets, premium organic stores nationwide, export markets

varieties.name: Sonalika
varieties.origin: Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR), semi-dwarf variety developed 1960s-70s
varieties.traits: Semi-dwarf bread wheat, high-yielding variety from Green Revolution; Adaptable to different agro-climatic conditions, resistant to various wheat diseases, medium-sized grains
varieties.yield_notes: High-yielding, played significant role in increasing India's wheat production

varieties.name: HI 8759 (Pusa Tejas)
varieties.origin: IARI New Delhi, developed for peninsular India
varieties.traits: Heat-tolerant bread wheat; Excellent heat tolerance, performs well under limited water, tailored for rainfed conditions, resistant to leaf rust and stripe rust
varieties.yield_notes: Consistent productivity in heat stress conditions

varieties.name: Raj 3765
varieties.origin: Rajasthan Agricultural University
varieties.traits: Drought-tolerant bread wheat; Drought tolerant, thrives in arid and semi-arid regions, good stability in low-rainfall areas
varieties.yield_notes: Stable yields in Rajasthan and Gujarat dry regions

varieties.name: WH 1105
varieties.origin: ICAR wheat breeding program
varieties.traits: Disease-resistant bread wheat; Excellent rust resistance package, suitable for various zones

varieties.name: HUW 234
varieties.origin: GB Pant University of Agriculture and Technology
varieties.traits: Late-sown, irrigated conditions wheat; Suitable for late sowing and irrigated conditions, early maturity (126-134 days), height 90-100cm
varieties.availability: Eastern Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Jharkhand seed networks

varieties.name: DBW 187
varieties.origin: ICAR-IIWBR/CIMMYT collaboration
varieties.traits: Early-sowing bread wheat; Can be planted as early as last half of October in northwestern plains, early maturity helps escape terminal heat stress
